passages.title: MENTAL GYMNASTICS
passages.source_html:
MENTAL GYMNASTICS


John McCrone explains how mental exercise is being used to boost corporate brainpower


A


The working day has just started at the head office of Barclays Bank in London. Seventeen staff are helping themselves to a buffet breakfast as young psychologist Sebastian Bailey enters the room to begin the morning's training session. But this is no ordinary training session. He is here, not to sharpen their finance or management skills, but to exercise their brains. Today's workout, organized by a company called Mind Gym, is entitled Having Presence'.


B

What follows is an intense 90-minute session, in which this rather abstract concept is broken down into a concrete set of feelings, mental tricks and behaviours. At one point, the bankers are instructed to shut their eyes and visualise themselves filling the room and then, the building. They finish by walking around the room acting out various levels of 'presence', from low-key to over-the-top.


C


Similar mental workouts are happening in corporate seminar rooms around the globe. Mind Gym alone offers some 70 different sessions, including ones on mental stamina and creativity for logical thinkers. Other outfits draw more directly on the exercise analogy, offering 'neurobics' courses with names like 'brain sets' and 'cerebral fitness'. Whatever the style, the companies' sales pitch is similar: 'Follow our routines,' they tell us, 'to shape and sculpt your brain or mind, just as you might tone and train your body.' Nearly all claim that their mental workouts draw on serious research into how the brain works.


D 


Gessner Geyer, from Brainergy of Cambridge, Massachusetts, puts it like this: 'Studies have shown that mental exercise can cause changes in brain anatomy and brain chemistry, which promote increased mental efficiency and clarity. The neuroscience behind this is cutting-edge.' Mind Gym trades on a quote from Susan Greenfield, one of Britain's best-known neuroscientists: 'It's a bit like going to the gym: if you exercise your brain, it will grow.'


E


 In practice, the training can seem mundane. Take 'Creativity for logical thinkers', one of Mind Gym's eight different creativity workouts. One of the mental strategies taught is to make a sensible suggestion, then pose its opposite. Asked to spend five minutes inventing a new pizza, a group soon comes up with no topping, sweet topping, cold topping, price based on time of day, flat-rate prices and so on.


F


The trick is simple but Bailey points out how few such tricks people have to call upon when suddenly asked to be creative: 'They tend to just label themselves as uncreative, not realizing that there are techniques every creative person employs.' Bailey says the aim is to introduce people to half a dozen such strategies, so that what at first seems like a dauntingly abstract mental task becomes a set of concrete, learnable behaviours. He admits this is not a shout cut to genius. Neurologically, some people do start with quicker circuits or greater handling capacity. However, the right kind of training he believes can dramatically increase brain efficiency.


G


Though it is hard to prove that the training itself is effective – how do you measure a change in creativity levels, or memory skills? – staff certainly report feeling that such classes have opened their eyes. For example, they may have felt the only way to solve a difficult problem is to bang away at it as hard as possible. Then they learn that creative thinkers advise taking a break and letting ideas incubate. A simple tactic, yet one rarely taught in normal life. Which, according to educational psychologist Guy Claxton, Mind Gym's academic adviser, is exactly the point.


H


Claxton, who dismisses most neurological approaches as 'neuro-babble', insists creativity, mental flexibility, even motivation are all thought habits that can be learned. The problem, he claims is that most of us never receive proper training. We develop mental strategies for tackling tasks haphazardly and soon lose sight of the very thought habits we are relying upon. Claxton believes we must return our thought patterns to a conscious level, becoming aware of how we usually think. Only then can we start to practice better thought patterns, until eventually these become our new habits.


I 


Russian psychologists Lev Vygotsky and Aleksandr Luria put forward similar arguments in the 1930s and various attempts have been made to put them into practice. The business world is certainly familiar with both 'better thinking' gurus such as Edward de Bono and Tony Buzan, and habit-breaking techniques such as neurolinguistic programming. Modern companies will seize on anything that claims to create flexible, bright thinkers, without scrutinizing the facts. But are neurotic workouts underpinned by scientific fact?


J


Certainly, the brain adapts to demands placed on it. Neurologists have proved time and again that people who lose brain cells suddenly during a stroke often sprout new Connections to compensate-especially if they undergo therapy. Rats raised in cages with toys sprout more neural connections than rats raised in bare cages. Brain scans suggest people use more of their grey matter to carry out new tasks than well-rehearsed ones.


K


So the general basis of neurobics looks sound: the brain really is inherently alterable or 'plastic'. The problem is in the specifics. Qualities such as creativity are too subjective to rate, forcing psychologists to rely on the easy-to-score spatial and verbal reasoning tasks involved in IQ tests.


L


This creates a problem for neurobics. Time and again, psychologists have found that neither mental exercises nor any other type of brain-boosting regime, including so-called smart pills designed to improve blood flow to the brain, reliably improve our ability to do these basic tasks.


M


Nevertheless, Claxton for one believes there is no reason why schools and universities should not spend more time teaching analysis and problem-solving techniques, rather than trying to stuff heads with facts and hoping that effective thought habits are somehow absorbed by osmosis.

groups.title: Questions 1-13
groups.source_html:
Questions 1-5


Choose TRUE if the statement agrees with the information given in the text, choose FALSE if the statement contradicts the information, or choose NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.


1

Bailey asks his participants to imagine they are buildings.

正确答案： B. NO

原文答案句：At one point the bankers are instructed to shut their eyes and visualize themselves filling the room and then the building.  

对应段落：B段  

审题：题目问“Mind Gym教练指示员工想象自己是建筑物”，即教练是否让员工想象自己成为建筑物。  

定位关键信息：利用题干中的"Mind Gym coach instructed employees to imagine that they are the building"定位到B段，找到教练指示员工"visualize themselves filling the room and then the building"的句子。  

解题思路：原文明确提到教练让员工想象自己"filling the room and then the building"，即先想象自己充满房间，再想象自己充满建筑物。虽然题目描述"imagine that they are the building"与原文"filling the building"在语义上接近，但原文强调的是"充满建筑物"而非"成为建筑物"，存在细微差别。因此，题目表述与原文不完全一致，答案为NO。


2

Brain training is used by business people all over the world.

正确答案： A. YES

原文答案句：Similar mental workouts are happening in corporate seminar rooms around the globe.

对应段落：C段  

审题：题目判断“脑力训练被全世界的商务人士所使用”这一说法是否与文章信息一致。

定位关键信息：使用 "all over the world" 和 "business people" (或类似概念如corporate, business) 作为关键词进行定位。  

解题思路：在段落C中，首句明确指出“类似的精神训练正在全球各地的公司会议室里进行着”。“全球各地”(around the globe) 对应题目的“全世界”(all over the world)，“公司会议室”(corporate seminar rooms) 则清晰地指明了使用者是商务人士。因此，该陈述与原文信息一致，答案为Yes。


3

The organisations that sell mental exercise all promote it in much the same way.

正确答案： A. YES

原文答案句：Whatever the style, the companies' sales pitch is similar: 'Follow our routines,' they tell us, 'to shape and sculpt your brain or mind, just as you might tone and train your body.

对应段落：C段  

审题：题目判断“销售脑力训练课程的机构都以大致相同的方式进行推广”这一说法是否与文章信息一致。

定位关键信息：使用 "organisations", "sell mental exercise", "promote it in much the same way" 作为关键词，定位到描述这些公司营销方式的句子。

解题思路：题目中的核心信息是不同机构在“推广方式”上的“相似性”。在段落C中，原文明确指出，无论这些脑力训练公司的课程风格如何，“它们的销售说辞是相似的”(sales pitch is similar)，并给出了统一的说辞范例。这与题目中“以大致相同的方式进行推广”的陈述完全吻合。因此，该陈述与原文信息一致，答案为Yes。


4

Companies offering mental workouts say that their practice has a scientific basis.

正确答案： A. YES

原文答案句：Nearly all claim that their mental workouts draw on serious research into how the brain works.

对应段落：C段

审题：题目判断“提供脑力训练的公司声称它们的实践有科学依据”这一说法是否与文章信息一致。

定位关键信息：使用 "companies offering mental workouts", "scientific basis" 作为关键词，定位到描述这些公司对其训练方法依据的声明的句子。

解题思路：在段落C中，原文明确指出“几乎所有的公司都声称它们的脑力训练借鉴了对大脑运作方式的严肃研究”。“严肃研究”(serious research) 直接对应“科学依据”(scientific basis)，而“声称”(claim) 也与题目中的“say that”一致。因此，该陈述与原文信息一致，答案为Yes。

5

Susan Greenfield was one of the founders of Mind Gym.

正确答案： C. NOT GIVEN

原文答案句：Mind Gym trades on a quote from Susan Greenfield, one of Britain's best-known neuroscientists: 'It's a bit like going to the gym: if you exercise your brain, it will grow.

对应段落：D段

审题：题目判断“Susan Greenfield是Mind Gym的创始人之一”这一说法是否与文章信息一致。

定位关键信息：用 "Susan Greenfield" 和 "Mind Gym" 作为关键词，定位到文中提及两者关系的句子。

解题思路：在段落D中，原文仅提到Mind Gym公司在营销中“利用”(trades on) Susan Greenfield（一位著名的神经科学家）的言论。文中只说明了她的话被该公司引用，并未提及她是该公司的创始人或与她有任何创立关系。题目信息在原文中找不到依据，因此答案为NOT GIVEN。


Questions 6-13


Look at the following statements (Questions 6-13) and the list of people below. Match each statement with the correct person, A-D Write the appropriate letters A-D in boxes 6-13 on your answer sheet.


NB you may use any letter more than once.


List of People

A Sebastian Bailey

B Gessner Geyer

C Susan Greenfield

D Guy Claxton


Question / ParagraphABCDEFG
6

6 People rarely take time to stand back from a problem before taking a decision.
✅ 正确答案：D

原文答案句：For example, they may have felt the only way to solve a difficult problem is to bang away at it as hard as possible. Then they learn that creative thinkers advise taking a break and letting ideas incubate. A simple tactic, yet one rarely taught in normal life. Which, according to educational psychologist Guy Claxton, Mind Gym's academic adviser, is exactly the point.

对应段落：G段

审题：题目要求将“人们很少花时间退后一步审视问题，再决定怎么做”这一陈述与列表中对应的人物匹配。

定位关键信息：使用 "stand back from a problem", "taking a decision" 以及描述人们不常采用的解决问题方式的句子进行定位。

解题思路：在段落G中，Guy Claxton指出，人们通常认为解决难题的唯一方式是“拼命硬干”(bang away at it as hard as possible)，而“暂停一下，让想法酝酿”(taking a break and letting ideas incubate)这种简单的策略“在正常生活中却很少被传授”(rarely taught in normal life)。这种“暂停一下，让想法酝酿”正是“退后一步审视问题”的具体表现。因此，提出这一观察的人是Guy Claxton，对应选项D。
7

7 Most people underestimate their potential to be creative.
✅ 正确答案：A

原文答案句：The trick is simple but Bailey points out how few such tricks people have to call upon when suddenly asked to be creative: 'They tend to just label themselves as uncreative, not realizing that there are techniques every creative person employs.'

对应段落：F段

审题：题目要求将“大多数人低估了自己的创造潜力”这一陈述与列表中对应的人物匹配。

定位关键信息：使用 "underestimate their potential to be creative" 以及描述人们对自己创造力错误认知的句子进行定位。

解题思路：在段落F中，Sebastian Bailey指出，当被要求发挥创造力时，“他们倾向于直接给自己贴上‘没有创造力’的标签”，并且“没有意识到其实每个有创造力的人都在运用一些技巧”。给自己贴上“没有创造力”的标签，正是“低估自身创造潜力”的直接体现。因此，提出这一观察的人是Sebastian Bailey，对应选项A。
8

8 Most ways of looking at mental development are unconvincing.
✅ 正确答案：D

原文答案句：Claxton, who dismisses most neurological approaches as 'neuro-babble', insists creativity, mental flexibility, even motivation are all thought habits that can be learned.

对应段落：H段

审题：使用 "most ways of looking at mental development" 和 "unconvincing" (对应 dismissive 或 critical 的态度) 进行定位。

定位关键信息：题目中的关键词是"Exercise can keep your brain health instead of improving someone’s IQ"，通过这些关键词可以在G段找到Katz的观点。

解题思路：段落H中，Guy Claxton将“大多数神经学方法”斥为“神经学胡话”(neuro-babble)。这种明确且强烈的否定态度，与题目中“认为大多数方法都缺乏说服力”的陈述完全吻合。因此，提出这一观点的人是Guy Claxton，对应选项D。
9

9 Exercising the brain can be compared to exercising the body.
✅ 正确答案：C

原文答案句：Mind Gym trades on a quote from Susan Greenfield, one of Britain's best-known neuroscientists: 'It's a bit like going to the gym: if you exercise your brain, it will grow.'

对应段落：D段

审题：题目要求将“可以将锻炼大脑类比锻炼身体”这一陈述与列表中对应的人物匹配。

定位关键信息：使用 "exercising the brain", "compared to", "exercising the body" 进行定位。

解题思路：在段落D中，Susan Greenfield被引用的原话明确地将去健身房锻炼身体与锻炼大脑会使它成长进行了直接类比。因此，提出这一观点的人是Susan Greenfield，对应选项C。
10

10 Training can actually modify the structure of the brain.
✅ 正确答案：B

原文答案句：Studies have shown that mental exercise can cause changes in brain anatomy and brain chemistry, which promote increased mental efficiency and clarity.

对应段落：D段

审题：题目要求将“训练确实可以改变大脑的结构”这一陈述与列表中对应的人物匹配。

定位关键信息： 使用 "modify the structure of the brain" (对应 changes in brain anatomy) 进行定位。

解题思路：段落D中，Gessner Geyer明确指出，研究表明脑力锻炼可以引起大脑解剖结构的变化，而且可以改变大脑的化学性质。这与题目陈述一致。因此，提出这一观点的人是Gessner Geyer，对应选项B。
11

11 Effective mental training begins with a close examination of how we ordinarily think. 
✅ 正确答案：D

原文答案句：Claxton believes we must return our thought patterns to a conscious level, becoming aware of how we usually think. Only then can we start to practice better thought patterns, until eventually these become our new habits.

对应段落：H段

审题：题目要求将“有效的心智训练开始于仔细审视我们惯常的思维方式”这一陈述与列表中对应的人物匹配。

定位关键信息： 使用 "begins with", "close examination", "how we ordinarily think" 进行定位。

解题思路：段落H中，Guy Claxton的观点清晰地描述了这一过程：我们必须“先让思维模式回归到意识层面，意识到我们通常是如何思考的”，然后“才能开始练习更好的思维模式”。这与题目的逻辑完全吻合。因此，提出这一观点的人是Guy Claxton，对应选项D。
12

12 People who think that they are uncreative may be unaware of techniques used by creative people.
✅ 正确答案：A

原文答案句：The trick is simple but Bailey points out how few such tricks people have to call upon when suddenly asked to be creative: 'They tend to just label themselves as uncreative, not realizing that there are techniques every creative person employs.' 

对应段落：F段

审题： 题目要求将“那些认为自己没有创造力的人可能不知道有创造力的人所使用的技巧”这一陈述与列表中对应的人物匹配。

定位关键信息：使用 "unaware of techniques used by creative people" 进行定位。

解题思路：段落F中，Sebastian Bailey指出，人们倾向于给自己贴上“没有创造力”的标签，同时“没有意识到每个有创造力的人都在使用技巧”。这与题目陈述是完全一致的同义转述。因此，提出这一观点的人是Sebastian Bailey，对应选项A。
13

13 Educators should place greater emphasis on developing students' thinking skills.
✅ 正确答案：D

原文答案句：Nevertheless, Claxton for one believes there is no reason why schools and universities should not spend more time teaching analysis and problem-solving techniques, rather than trying to stuff heads with facts and hoping that effective thought habits are somehow absorbed by osmosis.

对应段落：M段

审题：题目要求将“教育者应更重视培养学生的思维技能”这一陈述与列表中对应的人物匹配。

定位关键信息：使用 "educators", "place greater emphasis", "students' thinking skills" 进行定位。

解题思路：段落M中，Guy Claxton明确主张学校和大学“应该花更多时间教授分析和解决问题的技巧”，而不是试图往脑袋里塞满死知识。这正是题目陈述所表达的观点。因此，提出这一观点的人是Guy Claxton，对应选项D。
